Skip to contents
isopleuros 1.1.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Ternary Plot Matrices

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/ternary_pairs.R
ternary_pairs.Rd

Produces a matrix of ternary plots.

Usage

ternary_pairs(x, ...)

# S4 method for matrix
ternary_pairs(x, margin = NULL, ...)

# S4 method for data.frame
ternary_pairs(x, margin = NULL, ...)

Arguments

x

A matrix or a data.frame. Columns are converted to numeric in the same way that data.matrix() does.

...

Further graphical parameters.

margin

A character string or an integer giving the index of the column to be used as the third part of the ternary plots. If NULL (the default), marginal compositions will be used (i.e. the geometric mean of the non-selected parts).

Value

ternary_pairs() is called it for its side-effects: it results in a graphic being displayed. Invisibly returns x.

See also

Other graphical elements: ternary_axis(), ternary_box(), ternary_grid(), ternary_plot(), ternary_title()

Author

N. Frerebeau

Examples

## Data from Aitchison 1986
## Ternary plots with marginal compositions
ternary_pairs(boxite)
#>  [1] 43.5 41.1 41.5 33.9 46.5 45.3 33.2 40.8 33.0 28.2 33.9 48.7 37.8 42.0 44.2
#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8
#>  [1] 25.1 27.5 20.1 37.8 16.0 19.4 25.2 15.1 30.8 38.6 31.5 19.3 37.1 26.6 26.5
#> [16] 23.2 28.1 26.7 35.3 36.0 22.5 33.5 16.6 19.0 30.9
#>  [1]  9.949411 10.184572 11.528027  8.994256 11.924076 12.293989 13.420671
#>  [8] 13.519763 11.689435 10.558880 10.998788 10.424100  8.171226 10.117676
#> [15]  9.443289 10.980008 10.198693 12.574636  8.961614  8.087742 11.897559
#> [22] 10.134061 12.741563 12.224228  9.992711

#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8
#>  [1] 14.7 13.9 20.6 11.1 15.6 14.8 15.2 21.7 15.1 12.1 15.4 13.4 10.4 13.7 12.9
#> [16] 20.6 13.0 13.6 11.2  9.4 18.7 17.7 16.9 13.4 11.9
#>  [1] 11.89185 12.78543 11.43399 13.53178 12.02513 13.45466 15.88400 11.98050
#>  [9] 14.82466 15.54361 13.96180 11.77216 12.48535 12.62218 12.00444 11.42377
#> [17] 13.18658 15.74537 13.13929 12.65374 12.65430 12.53547 12.66572 13.73318
#> [25] 13.73474

#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8

#> [16] 10.2 13.6 17.0 11.9  8.4 11.4  9.8 17.0 21.3 12.9
#>  [1] 13.52141 14.51485 14.05118 13.37303 12.37901 13.87338 15.27246 13.67237
#>  [9] 15.62358 14.77091 15.17241 12.68907 13.30186 13.79256 13.24692 14.43994
#> [17] 12.98974 14.61671 12.87643 13.13717 14.92395 15.26592 12.64084 11.76737

#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8

#> [20] 6.7 7.9 6.0 7.2 6.4 6.5
#>  [1] 15.45244 15.37058 16.62629 16.89809 15.28247 15.70842 18.71027 16.84987
#>  [9] 18.17073 18.74407 17.98888 14.03940 14.91541 15.64098 14.86057 16.95581
#> [17] 17.06329 18.34424 16.75637 14.16565 16.86461 17.97828 16.83247 17.56896

#> [16] 20.6 13.0 13.6 11.2  9.4 18.7 17.7 16.9 13.4 11.9

#> [16] 10.2 13.6 17.0 11.9  8.4 11.4  9.8 17.0 21.3 12.9

#>  [9] 15.98705 13.30336 15.54835 17.27509 13.38500 16.06081 15.70991 17.27161
#> [17] 14.52655 15.85857 12.98495 13.54981 18.00343 15.18959 17.26579 15.06905
#> [25] 14.29939
#>  [1] 25.1 27.5 20.1 37.8 16.0 19.4 25.2 15.1 30.8 38.6 31.5 19.3 37.1 26.6 26.5
#> [16] 23.2 28.1 26.7 35.3 36.0 22.5 33.5 16.6 19.0 30.9

#> [20] 6.7 7.9 6.0 7.2 6.4 6.5

## Ternary plots with endite
ternary_pairs(boxite, margin = "E")
#>  [1] 43.5 41.1 41.5 33.9 46.5 45.3 33.2 40.8 33.0 28.2 33.9 48.7 37.8 42.0 44.2
#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8
#>  [1] 25.1 27.5 20.1 37.8 16.0 19.4 25.2 15.1 30.8 38.6 31.5 19.3 37.1 26.6 26.5
#> [16] 23.2 28.1 26.7 35.3 36.0 22.5 33.5 16.6 19.0 30.9
#>  [1] 6.7 8.0 6.7 5.7 7.6 9.3 9.3 7.8 8.2 6.9 7.2 7.9 6.1 7.2 6.8 6.3 6.0 8.6 5.4
#> [20] 6.7 7.9 6.0 7.2 6.4 6.5

#> [16] 39.7 39.3 34.1 36.2 39.5 39.5 33.0 42.3 39.9 37.8

#> [16] 20.6 13.0 13.6 11.2  9.4 18.7 17.7 16.9 13.4 11.9

#> [16] 23.2 28.1 26.7 35.3 36.0 22.5 33.5 16.6 19.0 30.9

#> [16] 20.6 13.0 13.6 11.2  9.4 18.7 17.7 16.9 13.4 11.9

#> [20] 6.7 7.9 6.0 7.2 6.4 6.5

#> [16] 23.2 28.1 26.7 35.3 36.0 22.5 33.5 16.6 19.0 30.9

On this page

isopleuros is a part of the tesselle project, a collection of packages for research and teaching in archaeology. Learn more at tesselle.org.

Developed by Nicolas Frerebeau, , .

Site built with pkgdown 2.0.7.
